articles.title: Oil Prices Edge Down While Stock Futures Inch Up
articles.summary: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.

articles.title: 2 US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
articles.summary: Two U.S. service members are missing in southwestern Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (AFRICOM) said.

articles.title: In a Reversal, Doctors From Countries Under Trump’s Travel Ban Are Allowed to Stay in U.S.
articles.summary: In a reversal, the Trump administration has exempted foreign physicians from a visa application freeze that was pushing many out of jobs in underserved areas.

articles.title: Trump says the U.S will 'guide' stranded ships from the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United States will launch an effort on Monday to "guide" stranded ships from the Iran-gripped Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump said, as two ships around the strait reported attacks.

articles.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Help Stranded Ships Leave Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump offered few details on how the new program, which he called Project Freedom, would work, but said interference would be dealt with “forcefully.”

articles.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Florida in ‘Critical Condition’
articles.summary: A spokesman for the former mayor of New York City said Mr. Giuliani was critical but stable.

articles.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
articles.summary: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

articles.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
articles.summary: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Entrepreneurs fight pile-up of garbage in the West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli restrictions on movement in the West Bank are impeding garbage trucks from reaching landfills, leaving Palestinians living amid mounds of trash. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to cha

articles.title: Blasting beats and neon lights: inside Nairobi's 'nganya' bus culture
articles.summary: In Nairobi, the matatu isn't just a ride—it's a rolling nightclub, art gallery, and heartbeat of the city all in one.

articles.title: Modern economic chokepoints in war and the impact on geopolitics
articles.summary: Hoover Institution fellow Eyck Freymann explains what the Iran war and the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz reveals about modern economic chokepoints, geopolitics and war strategies.

articles.title: How does diplomacy work during a military deadlock?
articles.summary: Suzanne DiMaggio, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, explains the nuances of diplomatic moves at a time of military stalemate.

articles.title: Pellet found in Secret Service agent's vest links suspect to WHCD attack, Pirro says
articles.summary: The pellet "definitively" links the suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, to the attack, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro told CNN on Sunday.

articles.title: Trump Faces the Complicated Reality of a Costly, Unpopular War in Iran
articles.summary: President Trump’s predictions of a relatively short-term conflict with minimal economic consequences appear to be crumbling.

articles.title: Inquiry Into Fed Chair Jerome Powell Could Be Resurrected, Jeanine Pirro Says
articles.summary: Jeanine Pirro, the U.S. attorney in Washington, dropped an inquiry into Jerome Powell last month after a federal judge blocked her grand jury subpoenas.

articles.title: Blanche Says Others Who Post ‘86 47’ Message Won’t Be Charged Like Comey
articles.summary: The acting attorney general said such messages were “posted constantly” and said the Justice Department had gathered other evidence against the former F.B.I. director, but declined to describe it.

articles.title: 'I just want to see her again' says son of imprisoned Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi
articles.summary: On Thursday, authorities in Myanmar claimed they had transferred Nobel laureate Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest. Her son Kim Aris spoke to NPR about his doubts about the regime's account.

articles.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
articles.summary: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money ba

articles.title: A Long, Strange Trip: How the G.O.P. Came to Embrace Psychedelic Drugs
articles.summary: For decades, conservatives were adamantly opposed to the use of drugs like psilocybin and LSD. Now, the Trump administration has made a sharp pivot.

articles.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
articles.summary: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
